steps.text: Include the specified requirements and guidance in all procurement request packages, RFPs, and new or renegotiated contracts and/or permits.
Emphasize the purpose of the Food Service Guidelines for Federal Facilities in the Scope of Work language.
Indicate in the Scope of Work language that CDC is requesting proposals from experienced food service vendors.
Offer affordable healthier food and beverage options
Maintain operations following environmentally responsible practices and support communities through local food sourcing, to the extent feasible
Use behavioral design strategies to encourage the selection of healthier foods and beverages
Incorporate the Food Service Guidelines standards into the procurement request package, RFP Statement of Work, and contractual/permit performance requirements
Require the vendor to comply with food and nutrition standards in prepared foods, packaged foods, and beverages at the standard implementation level
Require the vendor to implement at least one standard from the behavioral design categories at the innovative implementation level
Require the vendor to comply with the standards included in each of the facility efficiency categories at the standard implementation level.
Require the vendor to comply with the food safety standards at the standard level of implementation.
Explicitly require adherence to all applicable state and/or local food safety regulations in the final contract.
Produce a reporting plan with monthly sales data and monthly or weekly operating statements.
Report key indicators to the assigned COR or project officer post award.
Develop a Procurement Request Package, with a Statement of Work, that reflects minimum requirements defined by this policy.
Process the Procurement Request Package submitted by the Program Official and take all actions on behalf of the U.S. Government as permitted by the Federal Acquisition Regulations.
Determine the adequacy of performance by the vendor in accordance with the terms and conditions of this policy and stipulated in the contract and/or permit.
Ensure compliance with requirements through periodic reviews and inspections.
Assist the vendor with the resolution of any issues that arise regarding performance or implementation.

Assist the Program Official, CO, or assigned COR or Project Officer in integrating Food Service Guidelines requirements into Procurement Request Packages, CDC-issued RFPs, and final approved contracts or permits.
Provide technical direction and guidance related to issues that may jeopardize the successful implementation of this policy.

preconditions.text: The contract or permit is not an existing food service contract or permit in place prior to adoption of this policy.
The food and beverages are not served at CDC employee organization-sponsored events, personal employee-initiated gatherings, or brought/paid for by an outside organization.
The food or light refreshments are not provided with CDC-appropriated funding.
The food and beverages are not sold and served as part of an emergency response or crisis.
The contract or permit has been awarded (post award).

constraints.text: Environmentally responsible practices are used in federal food service venues, and communities are supported economically through local food sourcing when feasible
Food safety practices are followed to minimize the risk of foodborne illnesses
Follow food safety procedures to minimize the risk of foodborne illnesses
The selected food service vendor must be able to meet all minimum performance requirements, as stipulated by this policy.
